original_prompt:
/oh-my-claudecode:ralph 

Excellent! Thank you for the fixes

There are a few things, however, that do not align with my design vision for the game.

### Floor navigation issues

Many rooms seem to have two doors, appearing on the East side of the room, and as such the player might think that either door advances them through the map, eastward.
However, that is not the case, and it looks like one door might send the player back, or perhaps take them to a totally different room.
This is made worse by the fact that the overall floor map looks a bit like a linear sequence of rooms, and that is not the intention.
Think of the floor map as a 2-dimensional grid, so that the rooms might have exits North, East, South or West. The floor generation should produce such layouts (and should also be configurable: the starting floors, for instance, might be a bit more linear, while others might be more laberynthic)
Following this, the room should be displayed with the doors to exit it in the right walls (North, East, South or West) depending on the floor map. So if a room is generated in the floor map, with exits North and West, then in the room viewport, the doors should be placed in the North and West walls (and they should consequently lead the player to the rooms North and West in the map, respectively)
In summary: the floor map should be properly 2-dimensional, and the placement of the doors in the room should match that of the map structure.

### Data Log scrolling issues

It does not seem possible to scroll the data log down at all. Make it so new information is appended at the _top_ of the log, rather than at the bottom, so the player always has the most recent information on sight.
